text_block_list.name: Monólogo
text_block_list.text:
Following the Kikuka Sho, Narita<n>Taishin spends
her days in a listless<n>melancholy, shirking both
training and<n>school—until her spirits are stirred by
a basketball game.

text_block_list.name: Monólogo
text_block_list.text:
She realizes that the young,<n>redheaded player on
TV was the one<n>who confronted her before the
Satsuki Sho.

text_block_list.name: Monólogo
text_block_list.text:
The girl tells of how she received the<n>courage to
never give up from a<n>certain person—someone
who is<n>stronger than anyone she knows.

text_block_list.name: Monólogo
text_block_list.text:
And Winning Ticket explains that<n>Narita Taishin's
strength to never<n>back down has always inspired
and<n>encouraged her.

text_block_list.name: Monólogo
text_block_list.text:
All this time, I never lose faith in Narita<n>Taishin and
wait for her return every<n>day—until I finally get the
chance to<n>give her some heartfelt<n>encouragement.

text_block_list.name: Monólogo
text_block_list.text:
She rediscovers her will to keep<n>fighting and vows
to make a<n>comeback at the Nikkei Sho.
